steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage narrates a historical event in 1613. The verb in the blank must match the tense used throughout: "ignited" is simple past, so the blank must also be simple past.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has malfunctioned': present perfect — describes an action with ongoing relevance to the present, not a completed 1613 event. ✗
  • Choice B — 'malfunctioned': simple past — matches "ignited" and correctly places this event in 1613. ✓
  • Choice C — 'will malfunction': future tense — cannot describe something that already happened centuries ago. ✗
  • Choice D — 'malfunctions': simple present — describes a recurring or current action, not a one-time historical event. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
